data.job_description:
About Us:

At Calfus, we are known for delivering cutting-edge AI agents and products that transform businesses in ways previously unimaginable. We empower companies to harness the full potential of AI, unlocking opportunities they never imagined possible before the AI era. Our software engineering teams are highly valued by customers, whether start-ups or established enterprises, because we consistently deliver solutions that drive revenue growth. Our ERP solution teams have successfully implemented cloud solutions and developed tools that seamlessly integrate with ERP systems, reducing manual work so teams can focus on high-impact tasks.

None of this would be possible without talent like you! Our global teams thrive on collaboration, and we’re actively looking for skilled professionals to strengthen our in-house expertise and help us deliver exceptional AI, software engineering, and solutions using enterprise applications.

As one of the fastest-growing companies in our industry, we take pride in fostering a culture of innovation where new ideas are always welcomed—without hesitation. We are driven and expect the same dedication from our team members. Our speed, agility, and dedication set us apart, and we perform best when surrounded by high-energy, driven individuals.

To continue our rapid growth and deliver an even greater impact, we invite you to apply for our open positions and become part of our journey!

About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Finance Manager to oversee and manage the financial operations of our company. Calfus is headquartered in the US and has India operations in Pune and Bangalore and a subsidiary in Canada. The ideal candidate will play a pivotal role in financial planning, analysis, and decision-making to ensure the company's financial health and long-term growth.

What You’ll Do:

Financial Planning & Analysis: -
• Develop and manage budgets, forecasts, and financial models.
• Analyze financial data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities to optimize costs.
• Provide actionable insights to support strategic decision-making.

Business Partnering: -
• Baseline key cost levers and collaborate with leaders to establish benchmarks for assisting in pricing, contract structuring, and margin analysis for large deals including project profitability and profit margin.
• Collaborate with delivery, sales, HR and other teams to support cross-functional initiatives.

Accounting & Compliance: -
• Oversee day-to-day accounting operations, including accounts payable/receivable, payroll, and general ledger.
• Spearhead month end, quarter end and year end activities including accruals & monthly analysis on the actuals vs provisions.
• Ensure compliance with India, US and Canadian tax regulations and financial reporting standards including coordination with auditors for periodic and annual audits.

Cash Flow & Risk Management: -
• Monitor and manage cash flow to ensure liquidity and operational efficiency.
• Identify financial risks and implement mitigation strategies.

Stakeholder Communication: -
• Prepare and present financial reports to senior management and closely work with C-suit and top management directly.
• Collaborate with practice heads to align financial goals with business objectives.

On your first day, we'll expect you to have:
• Chartered Accountant with 8+ years of experience in financial management, preferably in the technology services industry.
• Proficiency in financial software (e.G., QuickBooks, Zoho or similar).
• Excellent communication and leadership abilities.
• Strong analytical skills and detail orientation. Ability to interpret data, generate and communicate insights. Proficient in advanced Microsoft Excel techniques.
• Effective written and oral communication skills with the ability to present complex information in a clear and concise manner.
• Ability to work independently, maintain confidentiality and collaborate effectively in a team environment.
• Individual will need to work across US and India time-zones.

Benefits:

At Calfus, we value our employees and offer a strong benefits package. This includes medical, group, and parental insurance, coupled with gratuity and provident fund options. Further, we support employee wellness and provide birthday leave as a valued benefit.

Calfus Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer:

Calfus Inc. is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes candidates from all backgrounds to apply. We encourage applications from individuals with diverse experiences and perspectives, as we believe in the power of a diverse and inclusive workforce to drive innovation and success.

data.job_title: Vice President, Financial Planning & Analysis Manager
data.job_description:
VICE PRESIDENT – ASSET SERVICING, FINANCIAL PLANNING & ANALYSIS

At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide.

Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what #LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary.

We’re seeking a future team member for the role of VICE PRESIDENT to join our Asset Servicing finance team. This role is located in Pune.

In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways:
• Serve as the core member of the Asset servicing reporting team
• Perform analyses—both historical and predictive—to support a range of finance processes. This includes period close activities, evaluating forecasted revenue and expenses against actual results, and delivering metrics and scorecard reports. Identify trends and present findings to finance leadership or other relevant business stakeholders.
• Lead the selection of methodologies and key assumptions in preparing short-term and long-range forecasts, ensuring these reflect shifts in business strategy and competitive intelligence impacting different business lines.
• Participate in developing annual operating plans. Gather and analyze financial information, conduct evaluations when necessary, and communicate findings and implications to senior leaders.
• Analyze the financial impact of new products and initiatives within Product Groups.
• Conduct advanced trend analysis on financial data and operational performance concerning highly complex topics.
• Contribute to the advancement of the finance team's strategic objectives
• Develop and implement process improvements leveraging automation and AI to enhance team efficiency and service standards.
• Attend governance meetings and forums as required.
• Build effective relationships across all Finance and relevant functional teams.
• Oversee the management unit hierarchy for the Product Group
• Take part in ad hoc projects as needed.

To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following:
• MBA or CA with 8-12 years of total work experience preferred; experience in financial planning and analysis preferred
• Team management experience preferred, but not required
• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills
• Good judgement, maturity and poise to enable interaction/influencing of senior management
• Thoughtful analytical skills; able to develop, clearly present and draw conclusions
• Self-starter: ability to prioritize, multi-task and meet deadlines
• Disciplined approach to managing processes and controls
• Energetic, self-motivated and effective under pressure
• Strong analytical and data manipulation skills; proficient in Microsoft Office – Excel, PowerPoint
• Preferred - Practical knowledge of building Power BI dashboards and/or AI solutions

At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at:

BNY Newsroom

BNY LinkedIn

Here’s a few of our recent awards:
• America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025
• World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025
• “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025

Our Benefits and Rewards:

BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay-for-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter.

BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer - Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

Description

About B S R & Co. LLP

B S R & Co. LLP is a member of the B S R & Affiliates network of firms registered with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India. B S R & Co LLP has its presence in 14 cities in India with over 120 partners and an employee strength of over 5,000 professionals. The firm is engaged in audit, other assurance and taxation services. The firm has a client base spanning Indian businesses, multinationals, and listed companies in India across industry sectors. The endeavor is to continuously keep raising the bar in terms of audit quality, experience for our people, and adoption of better technology.

An independent audit of financial statements is essential for the effective operation of business entities and the capital markets. We are committed to providing a constructive and challenging audit, one that is also independent and delivers the messages that matter to management and the audit committee. Apart from financial statements audit (including IFRS and US GAAP reporting), we also provide other forms of assurance and attestation reporting. Should be from the Integrated Professional Competence Course (IPCC).Should have cleared both the groups of IPCC and be eligible to start article ship as per Institute rules. Should have strong inter personal skills & communication - both verbal and written Candidates awaiting results can also undergo the evaluation, registration of articleship will be subject to IPCC results.

Equal employment chance information

B S R & Co. LLP has a policy of providing equal opportunity for all applicants and employees regardless of their color, caste, religion, age, sex/gender, national origin, citizenship, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability or other legally protected status. B S R & Co. LLP values diversity and we request you to submit the details below to support us in our endeavor for diversity. Providing the below information is voluntary and refusal to submit such information will not be prejudicial to you.

Qualifications
CA Intern

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst (FP&A)

Job Description:

Qualys is looking for a Senior Financial Analyst with strong Excel and financial modeling skills to support cost management across the business. This role partners closely with FP&A and business teams on budgeting, forecasting, and long‑range planning, leveraging regular forecast updates to deliver accurate forecasts and actionable insights to the Manager/Team lead.

What you’ll do:
• Support with annual budget and rolling forecasts
• Manage headcount and vendor cost forecasting by handling day-to-day activities related to forecasting/planning as required on a regular basis
• Support month‑end close (accruals, reclasses, HC role forward, allocations, additional tasks, as required)
• Assist/Build forecast models, review decks, and analytics tools
• Partner cross‑functionally, including with US (PST) stakeholders as required.
• Perform ad hoc financial analysis, tool/report testing, and drive process improvements - short-term and long-term.
• Over time, the role will require the ability to take ownership and be responsible for complex analyses/projects, as required.

What we’re looking for:
• Master’s in Finance/Accounting (or related)
• 3–6 years of FP\ &A, accounting, or analyticalexperience
• Advanced Excel skills;
modeling experience required
• Strong understanding of financial statements and cost drivers
• Experience with NetSuite, Coupa, Anaplan, and Power Query is a plus
• Detail‑oriented, proactive, strong communication, and an observant team player
• Interested in learning AI and new analytical tools, and assisting in the development and testing of new automation projects.

Location : Pune (Hybrid – 3 days office/2 days WFH)

Flexibility : During quarter end, month end and overlap with PST, as required

Role Overview: The General Manager – Finance & Accounts will lead the entire finance function including financial planning, accounting, compliance, taxation, treasury, audits, and investor reporting. This role will act as a strategic partner to leadership, ensuring financial health, scalability, and strong governance
frameworks across the organization.

Job Title: General Manager – Finance & Accounts
Location: Shivaji Nagar, Pune

Qualifications & Experience:
Qualified CA with 3+ years of experience in finance & accounts leadership roles.
Experience in real estate, hospitality, co-living, startup, or asset-heavy business preferred.
Solid experience in fundraising, audits, and financial strategy.

Key Responsibilities:
Financial Strategy & Leadership
Financial Planning & Analysis
Accounting & Financial Control
Compliance & Taxation

Position: Semi Qualified Chartered Accountant
Location: Borivali
Experience: 3 years (Article ship completed / pursuing CA Final)
Salary: 3LPA - 4LPA
Job Summary
We are looking for a motivated Semi-Qualified Chartered Accountant to join our CA firm. The candidate will assist in handling GST compliance, income tax matters, audits, and financial advisory assignments. This role offers exposure to multiple clients across industries and an opportunity to develop strong professional expertise.
Key Responsibilities
- Assist in Statutory Audits, Tax Audits, and Internal Audits
- Preparation and filing of GST returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, etc.)
- Handling Income Tax Returns for individuals and companies
- Assist in GST assessments, notices, and departmental queries
- Preparation of financial statements and audit reports
- Conduct data analysis and reconciliation of accounts
- Maintain client documentation and compliance records
- Coordinate with clients for information and compliance requirements
Requirements
- CA Inter / Semi Qualified CA
- Article ship completed from a CA firm
- Basic knowledge of GST, Income Tax, and Audit procedures
- Familiarity with Tally, Excel, and accounting software
- Good communication and analytical skills
Contact on - /

We are seeking a talented and motivated individual to join our team as a Day Trader. As a Day Trader at Exvix Quantitative Research Pvt Ltd, you will be responsible for executing trades within the financial markets daily. This role requires a keen understanding of market trends, risk management strategies, and the ability to make quick, informed decisions in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:
• Execute intraday trades across various asset classes, including stocks, futures, and currencies.
• Analyze market data and economic indicators to identify trading opportunities.
• Develop and implement trading strategies based on technical and fundamental analysis.
• Manage risk effectively by setting stop-loss orders and adhering to proper risk management protocols.
• Monitor trade positions and adjust strategies as market conditions evolve throughout the day.
• Keep abreast of market news and events that may impact trading activity.
• Collaborate with our team of traders and instructors to share insights and best practices with our community of students.

Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in any field.

Proven experience as a day trader with a successful track record of generating consistent profits.

Strong analytical skills and a deep understanding of technical analysis indicators.

Excellent decision-making abilities and the capacity to thrive in a fast-paced trading environment.

Demonstrated commitment to continuous learning and professional development in the field of trading.

Benefits:

Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.

A flexible remote work environment with the opportunity to work from anywhere in the world.

Access to cutting-edge trading tools and technology.

Ongoing training and mentorship from experienced traders.

Career advancement opportunities within our growing organization.

How to Apply:

If you are passionate about trading and possess the skills and experience required to excel in this role, we encourage you to apply by submitting the form. Join us at World Trades Academy and take the next step towards a rewarding career in the financial markets.
• Exvix is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

data.job_title: Looking For Deputy Manager – Business Tax ( 3+ yrs post CA exp in audit firm , CTC up to 18LPA)  in Pune for Top Indian Accounting & Advisory Firm
data.job_description:
Our client is An Indian MNC Audit & Advisory Firm with direct operations in India, Middle East , Europe and USA. They are looking for below role

Position- DM – Business Tax (Direct Tax)

Location-Pune

Experience- 3+ years post CA qualification experience in audit / management firm

Education – CA(Qualified)

Salary- Up to 18 LPA

Primary Skills- Business Tax (Direct Tax), Tax Compliance, Team Handling,

Notice Period- At Earliest

Key Result Areas:

1. Operational Compliance & Advisory

- Preparation, review and filing of income tax returns for corporate clients both Indian as well as foreign

- Preparation (on exceptional basis) and review of monthly TDS working and quarterly TDS returns, quarterly advance tax working, SFT return, etc

- Handling of dividend assignment from end-to-end

- Providing advisory on withholding tax implications and applicable tax rates on various foreign remittances (Form 15CB issuance)

- Providing international tax advisory on matters like POEM (Place of Effective Management), PE (Permanent Establishment) risk exposure, etc.

- Handling corporate tax litigation including preparation of appeals, preparation of detailed submissions, research on legal issues up to the Tribunal level, etc.

- Handling departmental interactions including Filing rectification and refund applications, Order-giving effects, Stay applications, etc.

- Assisting partner in achieving their revenue target.

2. Team Management

- Training and grooming of team members for effective ground-level execution

- Speaker at internal training sessions

- Preparation of tax alerts on key direct tax updates and amendments

3. Business Development & Client Relationship Management

- Preparation of proposals/presentations for prospective clients

- Participating in meetings with key clients

- Building client relationship and becoming single point of contact for the client

4. Practice Management

- Ensuring timely billing of the clients on monthly basis

- Ensuring collections and follow-ups with clients for outstanding dues

- Assistance in preparation of Monthly MIS reports

Required Competencies, Skills, and Experience

• Qualified Chartered Accountant with 3-5 years of post-qualification experience in Direct Tax.

• Experience in handling international tax advisory matters like POEM, PE risk exposure, etc.

• Good writing and oral communication skills

• Knowledge of basic IT applications like MS Office, Tally, Winman, etc.,

• Ability to read and interpret various tax treaties,

• Experience in handling litigation matters,

• Experience in handling team

Job Details

About the job

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Finance & Accounting professional to manage end-to-end accounting, financial reporting, statutory compliance, and internal controls. The role is critical to ensuring accurate financial statements, regulatory compliance, effective cash flow management, and timely statutory and vendor payments.

Key Responsibilities

• Own monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements and close process

• Lead statutory, tax, and internal audits; ensure a strong control environment and full compliance with SOX and Internal controls over financial reporting.

• Manage direct and indirect tax compliance (TDS, GST, advance tax, corporate tax), working closely with internal team as well as advisors

• Oversee payroll accounting and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, NPS)

• Ensure strong balance-sheet controls, reconciliations, and cash flow management

• Manage fixed asset accounting, capitalization, and physical verification

• Oversee accounts payable, vendor reconciliations, statutory/vendor payments and travel & reimbursements

• Ensure compliance with RBI, MCA, FEMA, labor laws and other regulatory compliances and proactively engage with relevant stakeholders on upcoming changes

• Drive process improvements, controls, and reporting quality
• Serve as the primary Accounting and Finance point of contact (POC) for senior leadership, collaborating closely with Directors and HR Partners to provide oversight, guidance, and strategic support for departmental initiatives.
• Supports Intercompany(IC) billing and collection and ensuring elimination with IC by maintaining appropriate reconciliations.

Qualifications & Experience
• Chartered Accountant (CA) certification required.
• 12–15 years of experience in financial accounting and controllership with with at least 3+ years in a senior controllership or finance leadership role.
• Strong knowledge of Indian tax, regulatory, and compliance requirements
• Experience with ERP systems and internal controls
• Proven experience managing shared services or global capability centers supporting multinational operations is strongly preferred
• Public accounting experience at a Big 4 firm is strongly preferred
• Stakeholder Influence: Necessary for engaging with local/global leadership, local auditors/advisor, and regulatory bodies.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills; ability to present findings to executive leadership.
• This role has potential to grow to a regional controller role of South-Asia, so multi region experience is an advantage.

Schedule

Full time

Affiliated Companies
Affiliated Companies: CENCORA BUSINESS SERVICES INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED

Equal Employment Opportunity

Cencora is committed to providing equal employment opportunity without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or membership in any other class protected by federal, state or local law.

The company’s continued success depends on the full and effective utilization of qualified individuals. Therefore, harassment is prohibited and all matters related to recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, promotions and transfers comply with equal opportunity principles and are non-discriminatory.

Cencora is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities during the employment process which are consistent with legal requirements. If you wish to request an accommodation while seeking employment, please call 888.692.2272 or email hrsc@cencora.com. We will make accommodation determinations on a request-by-request basis. Messages and emails regarding anything other than accommodations requests will not be returned

data.job_title: Lead Analyst - FP&A and Power BI
data.job_description:
Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years

Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM
• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.
• Comfort working with large datasets and connecting to varied data sources.
• Basic understanding of financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet).
• Experience with data cleansing, validation, and transformation.
• Exposure to SQL or other database/querying tools

Analytical & Soft Skills
• Strong problem‑solving ability and high attention to detail.
• Good communication skills with ability to translate data into insights.
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

About the Role

Agni Infra is looking for a qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) with 2 years of skilled experience to join our finance team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial operations, ensuring compliance, and supporting the company’s growth through sound financial planning and analysis.

Key Responsibilities
Manage day-to-day accounting, financial reporting, and compliance activities.
Prepare and analyze financial statements, budgets, and forecasts.
Ensure statutory compliance with taxation, GST, and other regulatory requirements.
Oversee internal audits and implement financial controls.
Provide financial insights to support strategic decision-making.
Liaise with banks, auditors, and external stakeholders.

Requirements
Chartered Accountant (CA) qualification is mandatory.
2 years of post-qualification work experience in finance/accounting.
Solid knowledge of accounting standards, taxation, and compliance.
Proficiency in financial software and MS Excel.
Excellent analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.

Job Type: Full time

Pay: ₹50,000.00 - ₹60,000.00 per month

Work Location: In person

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:
• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

data.job_title: Analyst Senior, Business Analysis (capital markets, securities processing)
data.job_description:
Position Type

Full time

Type Of Hire

Experienced (relevant combo of work and education)

Education Desired

Bachelor's Degree

Job Description

As the world works and lives faster, FIS is leading the way. Our fintech solutions touch nearly every market, company and person on the planet. Our teams are inclusive and diverse. Our colleagues work together and celebrate together. If you want to advance the world of fintech, we’d like to ask you~ Are you FIS?

About The Role

The Business Analyst will work as a liaison between the product management and development team to analyze the business requirements, convert those into functional specifications and get projects ready for development. The candidate will also need to participate in integration testing and validating new functions to certify they meet business requirements. The role also involves working with the SQA team & the Release Engineering team to make sure projects are released to clients on time. The candidate needs to possess strong analytical skills, excellent communication abilities, and a deep understanding of business operations and the overall SDLC processes.

What You Will Be Doing
• Analyzing Business requirement
• Preparing Functional Specifications documents
• Functional/Integration testing
• Creating and maintaining project artifacts
• Project Implementation in co-ordination with SQA and Release Engineering

What You Bring
• Understanding of trading and settlement of Equities, Options and Fixed Income securities
• Knowledge of Order Management Systems & awareness on Back/Middle office functions
• Understanding of risk monitoring and margining methods
• Knowhow on options & equity strategy parings
• Knowledge of Agile/Scrum practices
• Knowhow of SDLC processes

What We Offer You

Prior experience as a Business Analyst involved in the US trading/margining systems is preferred.

Privacy Statement

FIS is committed to protecting the privacy and security of all personal information that we process in order to provide services to our clients. For specific information on how FIS protects personal information online, please see the Online Privacy Notice.

Sourcing Model

Recruitment at FIS works primarily on a direct sourcing model; a relatively small portion of our hiring is through recruitment agencies. FIS does not accept resumes from recruitment agencies which are not on the preferred supplier list and is not responsible for any related fees for resumes submitted to job postings, our employees, or any other part of our company.

#pridepass

data.job_title: Head Of Finance Ca Manufacturing/consumer Durables Pune (India)
data.job_description:
About the Company: The company operates in the consumer appliances industry, focusing on designing and delivering cutting-edge home and kitchen solutions for modern households.

Roles and Responsibilities:

A. Financial Control & Accounting
Own end-to-end accounting, GL, reconciliations, and month-end close
Ensure financial statements are accurate, complete, and timely
Lead statutory, tax, and internal audits
Build and sustain strong internal controls and documentation

B. MIS, Reporting & Business Intelligence
Design and own MIS across:
SKU-wise and category-wise gross margins
Channel-wise profitability (GT / MT / E-commerce)
Dealer outstanding ageing and credit exposure
Inventory turns, slow-moving, and obsolete stock
Lead budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis
Translate data into explicit, actionable insights for the business

C. Working Capital & Cash Flow
Own AR, AP, and Inventory governance end-to-end
Design and implement dealer credit policies and limits
Run rolling 13-week cash flow forecasts
Improve cash conversion as revenue scales
Partner with sales and supply chain to balance growth with liquidity

D. Taxation & Compliance
Ensure timely and accurate GST, TDS/TCS, and statutory filings
Oversee e-invoicing, e-way bills, and reconciliations
Work closely with external advisors on assessments and notices
Build a culture of disciplined, predictable compliance

E. Banking, Treasury & External Stakeholders
Manage CC / OD / LC limits, renewals, and negotiations
Build robust, long-term relationships with banking partners
Support debt planning and fund-raising readiness
Drive efficiency in treasury management and interest costs

F. Leadership & Governance
Serve as a trusted financial advisor to the CEO and leadership team
Bring an objective, data-led perspective to key business decisions
Build, mentor, and scale a high-quality finance team
Lead process and system maturity, including ERP and automation where relevant
Authority & Decision Rights
Set and enforce dealer credit limits
Place dispatch holds for overdue accounts
Approve capex within agreed thresholds
Direct access to CEO / Board on financial risks and opportunities

Mandatory
Chartered Accountant (CA)
10–15 years of relevant experience
Experience in manufacturing, consumer appliances, FMCG, or distribution-heavy business
Strong hands-on exposure to GST, audits, and working capital

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Details
• Financial Planning & Analysis: Input and manage financial data related to Plan, Estimate, and Long Range Plan (LRP) into our Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) system.
• Monthly Reporting: assist in preparation of monthly reporting packages and other business presentations as required; prepare ad hoc reports as needed.
• Analysis & Modeling: Evaluate, estimate, and analyze financial and operational impacts of new business prospects.
• Variance Reporting: Identify and report on variances/changes by reconciling data within various financial systems and data warehouse applications.
• Provide in-depth analysis of deviations.
• Expense Management: Oversee operating expenses for departments including operations, distribution, and commercial organizations that drive key distribution channels.
• Analytical Reporting: Generate year-over-year, quarter-over-quarter, and month-to-date analysis comparing actual results against Plan/Estimate/Prior year metrics for key expense accounts and business drivers.
• Collaboration With Finance Team: Partner with the Finance team to ensure timely updates to estimates and provide monthly financial reporting to senior management.

.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting or related field; CPA or CFA certification is a plus.
• Minimum of 3 years’ experience in financial planning & analysis within a large corporation or global business service environment.
• Proficiency in Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) systems and Workday.
• Strong analytical skills with advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with data visualization tools is advantageous.
• Excellent communication skills with demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across different teams.

What Cencora offers

​Benefit offerings outside the US may vary by country and will be aligned to local market practice. The eligibility and effective date may differ for some benefits and for team members covered under collective bargaining agreements.

Full time

Affiliated Companies
Affiliated Companies: AmerisourceBergen Drug Corporation

Equal Employment Opportunity

Cencora is committed to providing equal employment opportunity without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or membership in any other class protected by federal, state or local law.

The company’s continued success depends on the full and effective utilization of qualified individuals. Therefore, harassment is prohibited and all matters related to recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, promotions and transfers comply with equal opportunity principles and are non-discriminatory.

Cencora is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities during the employment process which are consistent with legal requirements. If you wish to request an accommodation while seeking employment, please call 888.692.2272 or email hrsc@cencora.com. We will make accommodation determinations on a request-by-request basis. Messages and emails regarding anything other than accommodations requests will not be returned

data.job_title: FS RC - FS FINANCE- Financial Transformation and Month End Operations - Manager-Agile
data.job_description:
At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all.

Position: Manager – GL Close-FP and A-Investment Accounting-Reg Reporting-Financial Accounting

Experience: 10+ Years

Overview:

We are seeking an experienced Manager with 8-10 years of progressive accounting and financial planning experience to lead financial operations and financial transformation projects. The successful candidate will oversee general ledger close processes, month-end accounting operations, investment accounting, and financial planning activities. The Manager will lead finance transformation initiatives focused on General Ledger (GL) Close, FP&A, and Record to Report operations within Financial Services firms. This role requires a strategic manager with extensive process expertise and proficiency in technology platforms (e.g., Oracle, Workday, Adaptive, PowerApps) to drive significant value. The Manager will leverage process improvement methodologies and technology as enablers to achieve operational efficiency and enhance accuracy through best-in-class processes and tools.

Key Responsibilities:
• Chart of Accounts Mapping & Redesign: Design, implement, optimize Chart of Accounts structures that align with reporting requirements. Lead CoA mapping during ERP implementations, ensuring seamless data translation. Develop dimensional hierarchies and account coding standards to support multi-entity consolidation and reporting.
• Management of GL Close Activities: Lead and manage the complete GL close and month-end accounting cycles, ensuring timely, accurate, and compliant financial statement preparation. Review journal entries, reconciliation procedures and account analyses to ensure financial statement integrity. Coordinate inter-company eliminations, consolidation adjustments and balance sheet reconciliations.
• Investment Accounting Operations Oversee investment accounting operations, including security transactions, valuations, reconciliations, and reporting. Prepare investment performance reports, reconcile investment holdings and manage regulatory compliance for investment portfolios.
• Transformation Initiatives: Support strategic finance transformation programs, including ERP implementations, system migrations, and digital process redesign.
• Comprehensive As-Is Assessments: Conduct detailed assessments of current Finance processes, including planning, budgeting, reporting, and finance close, utilize process mapping and modelling tools viz. Visio, ARIS and identify areas for improvement.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including IT, Tax, Treasury, and Operations, to drive integrated finance transformation and foster a culture of continuous improvement.
• Technology Transformation: Establish automated close processes and eliminate legacy systems through process redesign. Implement advanced accounting platforms viz. Workday, Workiva, Blackline, Tagetik, Trintech and spearhead finance transformation initiatives.

Key Skills:
• GL Close: General Ledger Management, Chart of Accounts Design and Month end close processes
• Investment Accounting: Strong experience in Investment Accounting, NAV calculations and securities management with valuation expertise
• Deep Understanding of FP&A Processes: Extensive knowledge of FP&A processes, including forecasting, budgeting, finance close, intercompany accounting, financial reporting standards, and management reporting.
• Finance Transformation: Proven experience in conducting As-Is and To-Be process assessments, process redesign and continuous improvement methodologies.
• Finance Systems Proficiency: Significant experience with Finance Systems such as Oracle EPM, Anaplan, Adaptive, Workiva, Workday, Blackline, Trintech, Tagetik and designing operating models that integrate people, processes, and technology.
• Stakeholder Engagement: Demonstrated experience in stakeholder engagement, facilitating workshops, and translating complex business needs into actionable solutions.

Education:
• Domain Expertise: Strong exposure to working in the Financial Services domain across Banking, Insurance, Asset Management, and Capital Markets.
• Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration. Advanced degrees (MBA or equivalent) are preferred. B.Tech or Engineering graduates with relevant work experience in finance or financial systems.

Experience Requirements:
• Extensive Experience: 10+ years of experience in finance operations, accounting, or transformation roles.
• Proven Track Record: A successful history of driving finance process improvements, optimizing close cycles, implementing automation, and ensuring compliance in GL Close, FP&A, and R2R functions.

EY | Building a better working world

EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets.

Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate.

Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

data.job_description:

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

We are seeking an experienced Senior with6-8 years of progressive accounting and financial planning experience to support our financial operations and contribute to financial transformation projects. The successful candidate will assist with general ledger close processes, month-end accounting operations, investment accounting, and financial planning activities.

Perform journal entries reviews, reconciliation procedures and account analyses to ensure financial statement integrity. EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

Company Description

Algoneeti is a forward-thinking organization focused on creating innovative financial solutions specifically for the stock market sector. Our expert-driven approach aims to assist clients in navigating the complexities of the stock market, while offering tailored strategies to optimize their investments. We are committed to fostering a dynamic, collaborative, and growth-oriented work culture to drive success for our team and clients.

Role Description

This is a full-time, on-site role for a Stock Market Sales Executive based in Wakad,Pune. The Stock Market Sales Executive will be responsible for identifying potential clients, building and managing client relationships, promoting stock market solutions, and meeting sales targets. The role also encompasses staying up to date with market trends, providing financial insights, and delivering exceptional customer service to achieve overall sales growth and client satisfaction.

Qualifications
• Strong expertise in sales, client acquisition, and relationship management
• In-depth knowledge of stock markets, financial strategies, and investment opportunities
• Effective communication, presentation, and negotiation skills
• Analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and attention to detail
• Proficiency in using financial software, CRM tools, and MS Office applications
• Ability to work in a collaborative, high-paced, goal-driven environment
• Prior experience in stock market sales or financial services preferred
• Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or related field is beneficial
What We Offer

✨ Competitive Fixed Salary + Attractive Incentives

✨ Performance-based Bonuses

✨ Complete Training & Market Knowledge Support (for Freshers)

✨ Fast-track Growth into Senior / Team Lead Roles

✨ Energetic and Growth-Oriented Work Environment

DESCRIPTION

Responsible for the preparation and analysis of financial and statutory reports at Legal Entity and Department level. Supports corporate functions, regulatory compliance requirements, and business stakeholders through financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis. Contributes to regulatory reporting including BRSR, RPT, MSME, listed entity compliances, and other evolving statutory requirements within the India region. Ensures integrity of financial data, supports audit processes, and drives continuous improvement in compliance and reporting processes.

Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Analysis
• Prepare and analyze financial reports for corporate functions and department managers.
• Compile and present legal entity-level analysis for board presentations.
• Prepare statistical reporting including financial performance analysis, variance explanations, and cost analysis.
• Interpret financial data by analyzing trends, risks, and opportunities to provide insights to stakeholders.
• Evaluate financial results against targets and communicate conclusions effectively.
• Support month-end close activities including data preparation and consolidation.

Budgeting & Forecasting
• Partner with assigned organizational units to develop annual budgets.
• Support expense and staffing forecasts.
• Assist in statistical and financial forecasting processes to align with business objectives.

Regulatory & Compliance Reporting
• Support regulatory reporting requirements including BRSR, RPT, MSME, and listed entity compliances.
• Lead BRSR reporting at the legal entity level, particularly Social and Governance parameters.
• Manage statutory audit of core BRSR parameters and coordinate reporting on value chain partners.
• Execute special projects to ensure compliance with the evolving regulatory landscape.
• Streamline compliance processes across legal entities within the India region.
• Maintain compliance calendar and ensure timely filings and disclosures.
• Monitor cross-functional stakeholder inputs and support frontend audit activities and reporting to authorities.

Business Partnering & Continuous Improvement
• Collaborate with stakeholders to drive performance aligned with organizational goals and strategies.
• Influence decision-making through strong financial insight and business acumen.
• Identify opportunities to optimize work processes and improve reporting efficiency.
• Support special projects and continuous improvement initiatives as assigned.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Skills & Competencies

Technical Skills
• Financial Analysis – Ability to analyze trends, variances, risks, and opportunities to drive business improvement.
• Financial Reporting – Preparation and presentation of financial information in compliance with regulations and stakeholder expectations.
• Budgeting & Forecasting – Supporting annual planning cycles and expense/staffing forecasts.
• US GAAP Knowledge – Understanding and application of US accounting standards and company policies.
• Regulatory Reporting – Knowledge of BRSR, RPT, MSME, listed entity compliances, and statutory reporting requirements (preferred).

Behavioral Competencies
• Action Oriented – Takes on new challenges with urgency and enthusiasm.
• Collaborates – Builds strong partnerships to achieve shared objectives.
• Communicates Effectively – Delivers clear and concise communication tailored to different audiences.
• Demonstrates Self-Awareness – Seeks and applies feedback for growth.
• Nimble Learning – Learns through experimentation and adapts quickly.
• Optimizes Work Processes – Drives efficiency and continuous improvement.
• Self-Development – Actively pursues learning and professional growth.
• Values Differences – Embraces diverse perspectives and cultures.
• Business Partnering – Influences stakeholders and aligns financial insights with business strategy.

QUALIFICATIONS

Qualifications

Education
• College, university, or equivalent degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field required.
• CA qualification or progress toward a professional certification is preferred.

Experience
• Minimal to some relevant professional work experience in Finance or Accounting required.
• Intermediate level knowledge obtained through training or on-the-job experience.
• Exposure to financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, or regulatory compliance is preferred.
• Experience supporting audits or working with statutory reporting frameworks is advantageous.

Job Finance

Organization Cummins Inc.

Role Category On-site with Flexibility

Job Type Exempt - Experienced

ReqID 2425180

Relocation Package No

100% On-Site No

Responsibilities

About the Role

Join a collaborative, high-impact team supporting the pension and asset management accounting operations of Principal Financial Group. We're looking for an accounting professional who can work independently, think analytically, and proactively solve problems. If you enjoy improving processes, strengthening controls, and bringing efficiency to financial operations, this role is a great fit!

As an Analyst – Accounting, you will work across a variety of accounting processes while developing a strong understanding of our end-to-end operations. You’ll research issues, resolve discrepancies, and support key financial reporting activities. Your perspective centered on process and curiosity will help you see opportunities to improve workflows and encourage continuous progress.

What You’ll Do

In this position, you will:
• Research, reconcile, and update financial transactions
• Process and post diverse types of journal entries
• Perform general ledger and balance sheet reconciliations
• Update and maintain transaction details in internal databases and applications
• Prepare financial reports, supporting schedules, and perform variance analysis

Qualifications

What You’ll Bring

You’ll thrive in this position if you have:

Experience & Education
• More than 2 years of experience in Finance or Accounting, preferably within an offshore setting
• A degree in Commerce, CA/ICWA Inter, or MBA Finance, or equivalent experience
• CA Articleship or background in an offshore environment (preferred)

Skills & Proficiencies
• Solid grasp of the accounting cycle and basic principles
• Ability to quickly learn new business processes
• Hands-on experience with AP/AR/GL activities
• Basic knowledge of invoice booking and payment processes
• Experience performing account reconciliations
• Proficiency in Excel and MS Office (Word/PowerPoint)
• Ability to learn and use accounting applications, ERPs, and reporting tools
• Strong analytical, problem-solving, and planning skills
• Excellent verbal and written English communication skills
• A collaborative approach with the ability to work effectively in a team

Additional Information

Shift Options

We offer two flexible shift timings:
• 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM
• 1:30 PM – 10:30 PM

Location

This position is based in our Pune, Maharashtra office.

Job Description

About Citco:

The market leader. The premier provider. The best in the business. At Citco, we've been the front-runner in our field since our incorporation in 1948 led to the evolution of the asset servicing sector itself. This pioneering spirit continues to guide us today as we innovate and expand, push beyond the boundaries of our industry, and shape its future. From working exclusively with hedge funds to serving all alternatives, corporations and private clients, our organization has grown immensely across asset classes and geographies. For us, this progress is a pattern that we'll only maintain as we move forward, always prioritizing our performance. So for those who want to play at the top of their game and be at the vanguard of their space, we say: Welcome to Citco.

About the Team & Business Line:

Fund Administration is Citco's core business, and our alternative asset and accounting service is one of the industry's most respected. Our continuous investment in learning and technology solutions means our people are equipped to deliver a seamless client experience.

Responsibilities

Your Role:
• You will produce accurate custom and regulatory risk management and fund performance analytics reports to be distributed to hedge fund clients, their investors and regulatory bodies in a timely manner
• Resolve all queries to risk reports
• Support the new business process - on-boarding new clients, assisting in the preparation of demos, marketing literature, maintaining demo risk system and product development (eg exploring/researching/bringing to market possible new revenue streams such as in response to emerging regulations)
• Be involved in the maintenance, prototyping and user acceptance testing of internally developed valuation models and risk tools
• Perform operational risk management - risk reporting process documentation, improving processes through increasing level of automation, ensure consistent application of CFS policies and procedures, identify and appropriately communicate potential internal and external risks.
• Assist relationship managers by participating in monthly calls or any escalation relating to day-to-day risk reporting issues, participate in communication/escalation aspects of complex issues resolution
• Contribute to cross functional training initiatives

Qualifications

About You:
• You have a quantitative background with a Bachelor/higher level degree or professional qualification (MSc, PhD, CQF, FRM, PRMIA, GARP, CFA, FIA)
• A quick learner who is self-motivated and demonstrates a strong attention to detail while multi-tasking
• Excellent oral and written communication skills and interpersonal skills
• Proficient in Excel, VBA, SQL and Python
• 0-2 years of experience in Financial Services, preferably with detailed knowledge of pricing/valuing/risk management of OTC derivatives using both in-house models/financial libraries/risk systems and specialist vendors such as Bloomberg BVAL, SuperDerivatives and IHS Markit
• Knowledge of investment risk measurement and management under regulatory frameworks such as Form PF, Form CPO-PQR, Annex IV, Basel III/CRD IV/CRR and Solvency II is advantageous

Our Benefits

Your well-being is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location.

We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect.

Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection.

He / She will also work closely with Functional and service delivery teams to ensure that all corporate submissions / deliverables are timely and accurate.

2. Expense Management

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities
Education

Experience

Knowledge and skills (general and technical)

• Good written and oral communication skills
• Strong understanding and comprehension of the English language
• Ability to establish priorities and handle multiple assignments concurrently with minimal direction in an evolving, fast paced work environment
• Demonstrate ability to work independently and in a team environment
• Self-disciplin ed and results oriented
About MetLife
Recognized on Fortune magazine's list of the "World's Most Admired Companies" and Fortune World's 25 Best Workplaces™, MetLife, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, is one of the world's leading financial services companies; providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management to individual and institutional customers. With operations in more than 40 markets, we hold leading positions in the United States, Latin America, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East.
Our purpose is simple - to help our colleagues, customers, communities, and the world at large create a more confident future. United by purpose and guided by our core values - Win Together, Do the Right Thing, Deliver Impact Over Activity, and Think Ahead - we're inspired to transform the next century in financial services. At MetLife, it's #AllTogetherPossible . Join us!
#BI-Hybrid

At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

Senior- FTS And V (Process/TOM)

EY – GDS – Consulting – Finance

As part of our EY - Finance Consulting Team, you will help clients develop their Performance Improvement initiatives and identify potential opportunities for performance improvements drive them. This includes techno-functional role conducting finance processes assessments, deep diving and analysing pain areas and coming up with solutions. The client base spans across industries and countries.

The opportunity

We’re looking for Consultant / Sr. Consultant with expertise in Process Assessment assignments with process mapping experience. Also, experience in FP&A, Financial Accounting and Reporting domain is an added advantage to join the global EY - Finance Consulting team. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a leading firm whilst being instrumental in the growth of a new service offering.

Your Key Responsibilities
• Experience with finance functions – ‘Record to report’, ‘Procure to pay’ and ‘Order to cash’ processes involving account reconciliations, Journals, Transaction matching, Variance analysis, intercompany and Fixed assets accounting / management
• Conduct workshops with clients to perform current state As-Is process assessment and design & optimize Future state processes as per leading practices & industry benchmarks
• Identify process improvement opportunities using automation, technology upgradation & process standardization, Finance Transformation and Operation model design and Assessments
• Create L1-L5 “As-Is” and “To-Be” process mapping using tools such as Planview, Nimbus, ARIS, IBM Blueworks and Visio
• Experience in implementation and development of process mapping tools for business functions
• Design KPIs and Control frameworks for O2C, P2P and R2R processes
• Strong accounting skills and understands financial reports and statements
• Knowledge on Financial Consolidation, Cost Allocation, Finance Planning and Budgeting Processes
• Should have knowledge of activities in financial close of an organisation for month / Quarter / Annual close periods
• Good Knowledge on ledger and sub-ledger systems; technical know-how of SAP ERP / HANA, Oracle GL (good to have)
• Translate business requirements to technical language and model (good to have)
• Good experience in Chart of accounts (CoA) design
• Managing/ supervising teams of people on project work
• Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in a high-pressure
• Open to travel (Domestic & International)

Skills And Attributes For Success
• Consulting experience is a good to have
• Confident & professional communication style
• Proficiency in English (oral and written)
• Ability to clearly articulate messages to a variety of audiences
• Flexible and adaptable; able to work in ambiguous situations
• Able to work effectively at all levels in an organization
• Capability to influence others and move toward a common vision or goal
• Must be a team player and able to work collaboratively with and through others
• Strong analytical skills as well as excellent problem-solving skills for interpreting the IFRS standards

To qualify for the role, you must have
• CA, CFA, CPA, MBA OR Master’s in business administration in Finance
• 1-2 years (for Staff level) and 3-6 years (for Senior level) of work experience in Finance domain
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills

Ideally, you’ll also have
• Exposure to tools like ERP, HFM, Visio, ARIS, BlueWorks, Celonis, Alteryx, etc.
• Project management skills, stakeholder management
• Finance Transformation

What We Look For
• A Team of people with commercial acumen, technical experience and enthusiasm to learn new things in this fast-moving environment
• Opportunities to work with EY Advisory practices globally with leading businesses across a range of industries

What Working At EY Offers

At EY, we’re dedicated to helping our clients, from world’s top companies — and the work we do with them is as varied as they are. You get to work with inspiring and meaningful projects. Our focus is education and coaching alongside practical experience to ensure your personal development. We value our employees and you will be able to control your own development with an individual progression plan. You will quickly grow into a responsible role with challenging and stimulating assignments. Moreover, you will be part of an interdisciplinary environment that emphasizes high quality and knowledge exchange. Plus, we offer:
• Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around
• Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career
• The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

data.job_title: Chartered Accountant/ Semi Qualified CA - Internal Audit
data.job_description:
Job Description - Risk Advisory/ Internal Audit

Position – Chartered Accountant / Semi-Qualified CA

Location - Pune

Employment Type - Full-time

Role Overview - Chartered Accountant (Fresher) or Semi-qualified professional with 2–3 years of relevant experience in Internal Audit, Risk Advisory, or related assignments. The candidate will support in conducting risk-based internal audits and assist in identifying process gaps and control weaknesses.

Key Responsibilities
• Assist in planning and executing internal audit assignments in line with audit scope and risk areas.
• Perform process walkthroughs, control testing, and documentation of findings.
• Identify gaps in internal controls, assess risks, and recommend corrective measures.
• Prepare working papers, draft audit reports, and MIS presentations for management.
• Coordinate with cross-functional teams and client stakeholders during engagements.
• Maintain updated knowledge of applicable accounting standards, Companies Act, and SEBI regulations (where relevant).
• Contribute to developing and improving audit methodologies and checklists.

Candidate Requirements

Educational Qualification
• Chartered Accountant – Fresher (preferred) OR
• Semi-qualified CA with 3-4 years of experience in audit/Risk advisory

Experience
• Hands-on exposure in internal audits, risk-based audits, or statutory audits.
• Experience with documentation, control testing, and audit working papers.
• Familiarity with IFC, SOX, or risk frameworks will be an advantage.

Skills & Competencies
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Good knowledge of accounting principles, auditing standards, and regulatory compliance.
• Proficiency in MS Excel, PowerPoint, and Word; knowledge of audit tools preferred.
• Ability to work under deadlines, manage multiple assignments, and adapt to dynamic work environments.
• Strong communication and report writing skills.
• Ethical, detail-oriented, and a team player.

Who we are?

Johnson Controls is the global leader for smart, healthy and sustainable buildings. At Johnson Controls, we’ve been making buildings smarter since 1885, and our capabilities, depth of innovation experience, and global reach have been growing ever since. Today, we offer the world’s largest portfolio of building products, technologies, software, and services; we put that portfolio to work to transform the environments where people live, work, learn and play.

This is where Johnson Controls comes in, helping drive the outcomes that matter most. Through a full range of systems and digital solutions, we make your buildings smarter. A smarter building is safer, more comfortable, more efficient, and, ultimately, more sustainable. Most important, smarter buildings let you focus more intensely on your unique mission. Better for your people. Better for your bottom line. Better for the planet. We’re helping to create a healthy planet with solutions that decrease energy use, reduce waste and make carbon neutrality a reality. Sustainability is a top priority for our company.

We committed to invest 75 percent of new product development R&D in climate-related innovation to develop sustainable products and services. We take sustainability seriously. Achieving net zero carbon emissions before 2040 is just one of our commitments to making the world a better place.

OpenBlue: This is How a Space Comes Alive

How will you do it?

The senior finance analysis is responsible for handling FP&A matters, including coordination with the functional department and business operations. He or She has the overall responsibility for preparing the HQ level reporting on a monthly basis and outlook rolling report by quarter and consolidated all HQ financial statements. He or she also has the responsibility of monitoring of the overall compliance with Johnson Controls´ finance policies, procedures and timelines applicable to the respective country’s legal entities.

Primary Duties:

Business partner

1. Review all of cost center and make appropriate accrual to ensure the P&L is fluctuated on a reasonable or explainable level.

2. MEC activities and variance to normal.

3. Monitor each cost center and make sure the allocation to be done.

Financial Reporting:

1. Assist the uploading work to HFM Essbase for yearly planning and rolling forecast on a quarterly basis.

2. Prepare and report to the HQ’ flash and outlook per timeline.

3. Provide the details actual data vs plan vs last year report to different functions.

Finance function:

1. Work closely with functional department for all end-to-end finance support, big projects’ expense and progress tracking to month-end closing.

2. Track to actual data with trend flux analyze rolling 3 months.

Skills and Competencies Required:
• Bachelor’s degree with accounting/finance major
• Previous financial and management reporting experience with an Multinational Corporate Finance or American company
• 5+ Years of relevant work experience
• Good command of English
• In depth knowledge of U.S. GAAP
• Good SAP knowledge with hands-on experience using the ERP
• Good Excel and Hyperion Financial Management system
• Excellent interpersonal
• Strong customer service focus

Accountant – Finance & Accounts

Role Overview

We are looking for an Accountant / Senior Accountant to support the India entity finance functions.

The role will involve monthly accounting, reconciliations, tax compliances and meeting other

regulatory requirements.

₹12,00,000 - ₹14,00,000 a year
Key Responsibilities
1.Accounting
•Prepare and update the books of accounts on a timely basis
•Reconcile GL balances and maintain proper documentation

2.Taxation
•Ensure timely calculation, payment, and filings for GST and corporate tax requirements
•Maintaining proper tax documentation (Form 16A, challans, returns, etc.)
•Coordinate with consultants

3.Audit & Regulatory
•Coordinate with auditors (statutory / tax / internal) for timely completion of audits
•Maintain audit schedules, supporting documents, and respond to audit queries
•Assist with corporate secretarial compliance and other regulatory filings

4.Miscellaneous Support
•Communicate with internal departments for resolving finance-related queries
•Verifying vendor balances, preparing financials, updating compliance trackers

Key Skills & Attributes

•Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) with 1–3 years of relevant post-qualification experience

•Strong understanding of Indian accounting standards, taxation, and statutory compliances

•Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Netsuite

•Detail-oriented, analytical, and strong communication skills

•Self-motivated, proactive, and able to manage day-to-day finance tasks independently

Preferred Background

•Prior experience in mid to large-sized organizations preferably in IT / technology sector

•Experience in accounting and tax is essential

•Exposure to facing audits and tax assessments

•Experience of transfer pricing will be an added advantage
